items.title: Mali : Bulletin Humanitaire (Avril - Mai 2026)
items.description:
Country: Mali
Source: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs

Please refer to the attached file.

Chères lectrices,

Chers lecteurs,

Ce bulletin humanitaire met en lumière l’immensité des besoins face à des moyens financiers souvent insuffisants et un accès parfois difficile aux populations. Malgré ces défis, les acteurs humanitaires continuent à se mobiliser afin de fournir une assistance essentielle à celles et ceux qui en ont le plus besoin.

Les résultats montrés dans ce bulletin (l’accès à l’eau, aux abris, le soutien aux moyens d’existence, l’agriculture résiliente, l’identification légale) renforcent la conviction que l’action humanitaire ne se limite plus à répondre aux urgences. Elle redonne l’espoir et renforce les capacités à préparer des lendemains meilleurs et stables.

Bonne lecture !

items.title: Philippines: 7.8M Sarangani Earthquake Flash Update No. 1 (As of 8 June 2026)
items.description:
Country: Philippines
Source: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs

Please refer to the attached file.

Highlights
• A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck 32 km offshore west of Maasim, Sarangani, at 7:37 a.m. on 08 June. Intensity VIII (very destructive) was reported in General Santos City. A tsunami alert triggered coastal evacuations across nine provinces and was lifted by mid-afternoon. More than 138 aftershocks have been recorded.

• Initial reports indicate 19 people dead, 12 missing and 134 injured. Figures for affected and displaced people remain provisional pending government validation and ongoing assessments.

• Homes, government buildings, hospitals and schools have been damaged or destroyed. Power and telecommunications outages have been reported, and damaged roads and bridges are constraining access to some communities.

• More than 3.2 million learners have been affected. Classes remain suspended in over 6,200 public and private schools pending safety assessments.

• The Government has activated national response mechanisms, including Red Alert status for the NDRRMC and the Inter-Agency Coordinating Cell. Search, rescue and retrieval operations are ongoing, while the United Nations and partners are assessing impacts and preparing to support the Government-led response.

Situation Overview
On 08 June, a magnitude 7.8 earthquake along the Cotabato Trench with a depth of 33 kilometers occurred 32 km offshore west of Maasim, Sarangani province, Mindanao at 7:37 am local time. Under the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS) Earthquake Intensity Scale the highest reported intensity is 8 (very destructive) out of 10 which was felt in General Santos City. More than 138 aftershocks have since been recorded ranging from magnitudes 1.3 to 6.7.

The earthquake prompted PHIVOLCS to raise a tsunami alert for the evacuation of coastal areas in Sarangani, Davao Occidental, Tawi-tawi, Sulu, Basilan, Zamboanga del Sur, Zamboanga Sibugay, Sultan Kudarat and South Cotabato. Similar alerts were also raised for Japan, Indonesia, and Australia. Tsunami waves of around 1 metre were recorded in Maasim and Kiamba in Sarangani province, and as far as Samar province in Region 8. By mid-afternoon local time, the alert had been lifted.

Based on initial reports, 19 people have been reported dead, 12 remain missing, and 134 have been injured. These figures, as well as the number of people affected and displaced, remain provisional pending government validation and the completion of ongoing assessments. Initial reports also indicate damage to, and in some cases the collapse of private establishments, government buildings, hospitals, and schools. Power outages and telecommunications disruptions have been reported in some affected areas. According to the Office of Civil Defense (OCD), access to some communities remains constrained due to damage to roads and bridges. Operations at General Santos International Airport were suspended following the earthquake and have since resumed at limited capacity.

The earthquake has affected more than 3.2 million learners, many of whom were due to return to school on 08 June. Classes have been suspended in more than 6,200 affected public and private schools pending safety assessments and further guidance from authorities.

items.title: Venezuela: Informe General del Encuentro Nacional del Clúster de Educación - “Conectar para Fortalecer” (18 al 22 de mayo de 2026)
items.description:
Country: Venezuela (Bolivarian Republic of)
Sources: Education Cluster, Save the Children, UN Children's Fund

Please refer to the attached file.

Durante cinco días, actores humanitarios del sector educativo en Venezuela se reunieron en un espacio estratégico de intercambio, aprendizaje y fortalecimiento de la coordinación.

? El Encuentro Nacional del Clúster de Educación contribuyó a:
✔️ Fortalecer capacidades para la coordinación de acciones del sector educativo
✔️ Promover el intercambio de experiencias entre territorios mediante presentaciones de contexto de cada estado
✔️ Reforzar la coordinación intersectorial a nivel nacional y subnacional
✔️ Integrar enfoques transversales, incluyendo protección, inclusión de personas con discapacidad, rendición de cuentas a las poblaciones afectadas y enfoques sensibles al género
✔️ Consolidar el posicionamiento de la educación —incluida la primera infancia— como una prioridad humanitaria para salvar vidas

El encuentro destacó la importancia de la localización, reconociendo el rol central de los actores locales y de los mecanismos de coordinación en terreno para una respuesta educativa oportuna, pertinente y sostenible.

Durante la jornada se compartieron experiencias y enfoques de respuesta desde distintos estados del país, incluyendo Zulia, Táchira, Amazonas, Falcón, Miranda, Distrito Capital, Apure, Bolívar, Sucre, Delta Amacuro, Aragua y La Guaira, fortaleciendo el aprendizaje colectivo y el anclaje territorial de la respuesta.

El resultado es una comunidad más cohesionada, preparada y comprometida con garantizar el derecho a la educación en contextos de emergencia.

? Más del 90% de las y los participantes indicaron que aplicarán los conocimientos adquiridos en sus respectivos territorios.

? El Clúster de Educación expresa su sincero agradecimiento a todas las organizaciones locales, nacionales e internacionales que hicieron posible este encuentro, así como a las y los facilitadores de los distintos sectores por sus valiosos aportes técnicos.
Un reconocimiento especial a UNICEF por su apoyo en la realización del evento, y a aliados clave como INEE, OCHA y las Autoridades Nacionales, cuyo compromiso fue fundamental para su éxito.

Reafirmamos nuestro compromiso de seguir trabajando de manera conjunta para fortalecer respuestas educativas más inclusivas, protectoras y efectivas.

#EducaciónEnEmergencias #ClusterEducación #Venezuela #PrimeraInfancia #AcciónHumanitaria ??

items.title: World: Show Me the Money: Efficiency and Effectiveness in Humanitarian Response
items.description:
Country: World
Source: CALP Network

Please refer to the attached file.

Executive Summary

The humanitarian sector has reached a critical turning point. Resources are shrinking, while needs are skyrocketing. Between 2024 and 2025, funding per person in need fell by 35%. To navigate this reality, the sector must fundamentally rethink how it maximizes the impact of available resources by embedding Value for Money (VfM) analysis as a strategic tool for cost-effective and accountable stewardship of resources.

This study used a three-pillar approach to assess VfM in humanitarian assistance. A desk review synthesized existing evidence on costefficiency and effectiveness. A qualitative review examined how VfM is applied in decision-making, exploring incentives, governance, data use, and organizational capacity through surveys and interviews with donors and agencies. Finally, the quantitative analysis drew on a cross-agency dataset covering 1,203 programs implemented between 2014 and 2025, with a total value of USD $11.4 billion. It compared the efficiency of transfer modalities and delivery approaches across Cash and Voucher Assistance (CVA) and in-kind food assistance, examining how program design and contextual factors influence the share of resources that ultimately reach people.

items.title: Ukraine: EU‐Funded Partnership boosts Health, Protection and Evacuations near the frontlines
items.description:
Country: Ukraine
Sources: EMERGENCY, European Union, Johanniter-Unfall-Hilfe, UK-Med

Please refer to the attached file.

A new international consortium has launched a coordinated humanitarian response to support people living within 0–50 km of Ukraine’s frontlines.

A new consortium of the German “Johanniter International Assistance”, the Italian “EMERGENCY”, the British “UK-Med”, and Ukrainian “Eleos Ukraine” has started their activities to support people living close to frontline areas in Ukraine. The partners will deliver an integrated package of primary health services, mental health and psychosocial support, energy resilience for health facilities, contingency planning and safe, dignified evacuations for the most vulnerable in Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts.

The EU Humanitarian Aid and Aktion Deutschland Hilft, Germany, fund these activities, which includes action targeting at‐risk populations within 0–50 km of frontline areas and prioritizes older people, persons with disabilities and those excluded from other assistance. It also includes pre-positioned supplies, emergency simulations and care-for-carers activities to reinforce local health system resilience. The partners aim to reduce immediate suffering, preserve access to essential health services and increase the resilience of health systems under strain.

Johanniter has the consortium lead and will focus on strengthening the energy resilience and winterization measures for frontline health facilities. "We are honored to launch a major initiative to provide critical aid across Ukraine, implemented in direct coordination with our partners and with the support of European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid. This project is dedicated to meeting the urgent needs of our communities by delivering life‐saving medical care, assisting with evacuations from frontline areas, and providing psychological support to those affected by the war," says Adam McKerrow, Johanniter Country Director for Ukraine.

The British UK‐Med will deploy four mobile medical units to expand access to primary health care in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia. “The need for medical support in Ukraine’s frontline regions remains extremely urgent. Attacks on civilian infrastructure not only damage health facilities but also displace healthcare workers and increase pressure on already overstretched hospitals. Thousands living near the frontline have limited or no access to essential healthcare services. International cooperation and strong partnerships remain essential to sustain this response," explains Lidya De Pascale, Head of Programme, UK‐Med Ukraine.

"Behind every humanitarian statistic is a person, a family, or a community trying to cope with the consequences of the Russian invasion. Through this EU-funded partnership, humanitarian organisations will provide critical support to people living near the frontlines, helping them access healthcare, protection services and safe evacuations. The European Union stands in solidarity with the people of Ukraine and remains committed to supporting those whose lives have been most affected by the war." — says Marianna Franco, Head of the EU Humanitarian Aid Office in Ukraine.

EMERGENCY will support primary health care (PHC) centres, referral hospitals, and community health workers to maintain essential healthcare services for displaced and host populations. Support includes disease screening and treatment, medical supplies, equipment, infection prevention items, and minor repair works.

Eleos Ukraine will lead targeted evacuations and provide legal, protection and psychosocial services on arrival. "Since the start of the full‐scale invasion, we have been working where help is needed most. In cooperation with Johanniter we will continue to evacuate families, provide psychological and legal counselling, assist with document restoration and support people with essential supplies. Together, we ensure assistance is timely, professional and compassionate”, comments Serhii Dmytriev, Chairman of the Board, Eleos Ukraine.

The project started on 1 May 2026 and runs until 30 April 2027.

For media enquiries and further information:

Johanniter International Assistance: Sandra Lorenz, Head of Communication

Phone: +49 172 563 8740, Email: sandra.lorenz@johanniter.de

items.title: Philippines earthquake on first day back at school affects 3 million students as rescue efforts underway
items.description:
Country: Philippines
Source: Save the Children

MANILA, 8 June - A magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked parts of the southern Philippines, causing damage in a key coastal city and affecting about 3 million students in the province of Sarangani [1]. Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. suspended school classes in affected areas while calling on residents in coastal areas to evacuate immediately after a tsunami warning was issued.

The country's Department of Education said that about 6,000 schools have been exposed to the earthquake, although this data could change as the full extent of the damage unfolds.

Faisah Ali, Humanitarian Manager, Save the Children Philippines, said:

"Today marked the opening of classes and schools across the Philippines after the country's summer break, but the Mindanao earthquake has put a terrifying and sudden stop to that. While the full extent of the damage is not yet known, our teams are reporting that the earthquake has destroyed classrooms and schools and forced classes to shut across many parts of the island.

Children are scared, and their lives and learning have been disrupted. We're assessing the situation on the ground, where Save the Children already has a presence, and will work to reach affected children as soon as possible, including to ensure that learning continues even after an event like this."

Save the Children has been working in the Philippines since 1981 with programmes in humanitarian response, health and nutrition, education, and children's rights and protection.

USD 4.5M required in the next six months (June – November 2026)

KEY HIGHLIGHTS

  • WFP Deputy Executive Director Carl Skau attended the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in Türkiye on 17–18 April. He spoke on two panels—one on prospects for a viable political settlement in Palestine and another on humanitarian access—and held bilateral meetings with Turkish Deputy Ministers of Foreign Affairs, as well as Türkiye’s candidate for FAO Director-General. Discussions highlighted Türkiye’s strategic role in humanitarian diplomacy and its importance as a key supply chain hub for WFP.

items.title: DR Congo: Ebola Outbreak: An Expanding Health Crisis with Geographically Differentiated Impacts (Third Edition – June 2026)
items.description:
Countries: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda
Source: Mercy Corps

Please refer to the attached file.

This edition highlights a rapidly deteriorating situation in eastern DRC, with confirmed cases rising from 282 to 515 between 30 May and 6 June, across 25 affected health zones. Ituri remains the epicentre, while confirmed cases in Uganda point to increasing regional transmission risks.

The note also underscores the wider operational and humanitarian implications of the outbreak. Weak contact tracing, insecurity, population movements, misinformation and community resistance continue to constrain the response, while movement restrictions are affecting humanitarian access, cross-border trade, cash availability, education and livelihoods.

items.title: DR Congo: Épidémie d’Ebola : Une crise sanitaire en expansion aux impacts géographiquement différenciés (Troisième edition, Juin 2026)
items.description:
Countries: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda
Source: Mercy Corps

Please refer to the attached file.

Cette édition met en évidence une détérioration rapide de la situation, avec une hausse des cas confirmés de 282 à 515 entre le 30 mai et le 6 juin, dans 25 zones de santé affectées. L’Ituri demeure l’épicentre de l’épidémie, tandis que les cas confirmés en Ouganda soulignent le risque croissant de propagation régionale.

La note met également en lumière les contraintes qui continuent de peser sur la riposte, notamment l’insuffisance du suivi des contacts, l’insécurité, les mouvements de population, la désinformation et les résistances communautaires. Les restrictions de mouvement affectent par ailleurs l’accès humanitaire, les échanges transfrontaliers, l’accès aux liquidités, l’éducation et les moyens de subsistance.

items.title: Philippines: Flash Update: No. 1 – M7.8 Earthquake in Sarangani (8 June 2026)
items.description:
Country: Philippines
Source: ASEAN Coordinating Centre for Humanitarian Assistance

Please refer to the attached file.

OVERVIEW: On 08 June 2026, PHIVOLCS reported a magnitude 7.8 earthquake (depth 33 km) at 0637 HRS UTC+7 (0737 HRS local PH time), with the epicentre located 32 km south (S04°W) of Maasim, Sarangani Province, offshore southern Mindanao. The earthquake was initially recorded at magnitude 7.0 with a depth of 10 km before being upgraded in a second advisory to magnitude 7.8 at a depth of 33 km. PHIVOLCS assessed the quake as tectonic in origin, with damage and aftershocks expected. The strongest reported intensity was Intensity VII in the City of General Santos.

TSUNAMI: PHIVOLCS raised a tsunami warning, advising that wave heights of more than one metre above normal tides were expected, with first waves arriving between 0737 and 0937 HRS local time and potentially continuing for hours. Residents of at least nine Mindanao provinces were advised to evacuate coastal areas immediately. BMKG also issued a tsunami warning for the Maluku Islands, Sulawesi and Kalimtantan. Indonesia’s BMKG reported sea-level changes of less than one metre so far.

IMPACTS AND DAMAGES: NDRRMC Philippines and BNPB Indonesia are currently conducting initial assessments to gather information on impacts and damages. Initial news reports show damaged buildings and infrastructures.

RESPONSE EFFORTS:

  • The Philippine President directed all relevant government agencies to act immediately and urged residents in affected provinces to move to higher ground, stating the national government is coordinating with regional offices and local chief executives.
  • NDRRMC Philippines is conducting an emergency coordination meeting on the initial impact reports and the ongoing response operations. The NDRRMEOC is on RED alert.
  • Several ASEAN Member States indicated readiness to support through the AHA Centre.

The AHA Centre EOC alert level is raised to ORANGE, and continues to closely monitor developments in coordination with NDRRMC and BNPB, and remains on standby should the affected countries welcome assistance from ASEAN.

DATA SOURCES

ASEAN Disaster Monitoring & Response System (DMRS), ASEAN Disaster Information Network (ADINet); ASEAN Specialised Meteorological Centre (ASMC);

Indonesia: BNPB, BMKG;

Philippines: NDRRMC, PHIVOLCS;

Verified news media agencies

items.title: UNHCR Romania Ukraine Refugee Weekly Situation Update #199 - 8 June 2026
items.description:
Countries: Romania, Ukraine
Source: UN High Commissioner for Refugees

Please refer to the attached file.

Response and Advocacy

On 3 June, UNHCR conducted information sessions for Ukrainian refugees in Constanța, Bucharest and online. UNHCR delivered two sessions in Constanța County. The first session, organized in collaboration with the General Directorate of Social Assistance and Child Protection (DGASPC) Constanța, covered procedures for obtaining disability certificates, helping participants better understand eligibility requirements and available services. The second session provided guidance on eligibility conditions, application steps and support available under the Minimum Inclusion Income (VMI) scheme. UNHCR also held a session in Bucharest facilitated by the Jesuit Refugee Service (JRS) Romania on the rights and obligations of landlords and tenants, which outlined the relevant legal framework and helped refugees safeguard their housing rights and prevent potential disputes.

items.title: Philippines: Magnitude 7.8 Earthquake Rocks Mindanao on First Day of School Opening
items.description:
Country: Philippines
Source: World Vision

A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Mindanao on Monday, June 8, as millions of children were set to return to school for the opening of the academic year.

“We are continually monitoring the safety of children, their families, and all staff in the affected areas to ensure that everyone is accounted for. Many in Davao, Kidapawan, and Sultan Kudarat remain shaken and are experiencing dizziness following the earthquake and aftershocks, given our proximity to General Santos. We continue to prioritize the safety of children and staff while closely monitoring the situation on the ground,” said Elizabeth Delgado, World Vision Operations Manager for Mindanao.

The United States Geological Survey (USGS) reported that the earthquake occurred at a depth of 35 kilometers.

“I felt afraid at that time. We were in the middle of our flag-raising ceremony when the earthquake happened. Even though we were scared, we tried to cope by laughing. Some of our classmates were crying, and one even fainted. But the teachers helped us stay safe—they instructed us to do ‘duck, cover, and hold,’” said Kurt, 16, a World Vision-assisted child from Kidapawan City.

“When the shaking stopped, including the aftershocks, the teachers sent us home. It felt like the ground was swaying, making it difficult for us to even stand,” he added.

According to the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS), the earthquake’s epicenter was located 13 kilometers southwest of General Santos City, Sarangani. Strong tremors were felt across several provinces in Mindanao, including Sultan Kudarat, Davao, Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga Sibugay, and Surigao del Norte.

The strong tremor also prompted a tsunami warning from PHIVOLCS, advising residents in coastal provinces—including Sarangani, Davao Occidental, Tawi-Tawi, Sulu, Basilan, Zamboanga del Sur, Zamboanga Sibugay, Sultan Kudarat, and South Cotabato—to immediately evacuate to higher ground.

Arnann Jay Agosto, World Vision Field and Emergency Communications Officer, shared his firsthand experience during the earthquake.

“I suddenly woke up because of how strong the shaking was. I was sleeping in the living room, so I immediately woke my sibling, and we ran downstairs from the fourth floor. I left everything behind—my phone and my bag—and ran down barefoot. While running, I noticed falling debris, so my first instinct was to reach the safest area,” Agosto shared.

“There are still aftershocks, and there is currently no electricity. All work and school classes have been suspended,” he added.

“In disasters of this magnitude, children are often the most vulnerable. The psychological impact of sudden evacuations, combined with the risk of severe aftershocks, poses an immediate threat to their well-being. World Vision Philippines is closely monitoring the situation and coordinating with local government units to assess the immediate needs of affected families. We stand ready to respond with emergency relief and child protection interventions,” said World Vision National Director Dr. Herbert Carpio.

The earthquake occurred as the Philippines prepared to welcome an estimated 26 to 28 million students for the opening of classes in both public and private schools.

At the same time, Mindanao is experiencing rainy conditions, as the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) recently announced the onset of the wet season due to the southwest monsoon.

Mindanao was previously affected by a series of earthquakes in October, during which World Vision provided education and psychosocial support to affected children and families.

For media interviews:

Lei Kimmayong

Communications Manager

Lei_kimmayong@wvi.org; +63 9499964619

items.title: Mozambique: Displacement in Cabo Delgado Province Situation Report No. 1, As of 31 May 2026 [EN]
items.description:
Country: Mozambique
Source: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs

Please refer to the attached files.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • More than 20,875 people displaced by escalating violence in Cabo Delgado since May due to attacks or fear of attacks by non-State armed groups (NSAGs) across Ancuabe, Chiure, Namuno, Metuge, Mocímboa da Praia, and Montepuez districts.
  • Nearly all displaced families have fled multiple times, highlighting protracted nature of the crisis.
  • Humanitarian needs continue to outpace available resources. Overcrowding is straining shelter capacity, water and sanitation facilities, and health services, while increasing protection concerns.
  • Protection Humanitarian risks remain partners high. report heightened risks of family separation, gender-based violence, loss of civil documentation, regarding displacement, the and uncertainty duration particularly of among women, children, older persons, and other vulnerable groups.
  • Insecurity continues to disrupt humanitarian operations. Intermittent attacks and security incidents along key transport corridors, including the N380, N14, R763, R762, R760 and N1 roads, have periodically suspended movements and disrupted the delivery of humanitarian assistance.

Situation overview

Since the NSAG attacks in Ancuabe district in April, violence has intensified and expanded to several villages within the district, while also spreading to the neighbouring districts of Chiure, Montepuez, Metuge, and Namuno throughout May. As of 20 May, the IOM DTM had recorded 15,286 displaced individuals (4,886 households) residing in host communities and temporary displacement sites. The largest concentrations of displaced people were reported in Nanjua A and Nanjua B in Ancuabe district, hosting 5,206 individuals, followed by Namanhumbir Sede in Montepuez district with 4,215 individuals, and Maningane Centre in Chiure district with 1,877 individuals. The remaining displaced population is dispersed across multiple host communities in Ancuabe, Chiure, Montepuez, Metuge, and Namuno districts, complicating beneficiary registration and humanitarian response efforts, as partners must simultaneously address needs across numerous locations with differing operational and protection dynamics.

items.title: Ukraine: Tranziția de la Protecția Temporară: Perspective privind Șederea, Căile Legale și Opțiunile de Politici pentru Refugiații din Ucraina [RO]
items.description:
Countries: Ukraine, World
Source: UN High Commissioner for Refugees

Please refer to the attached file.

CONTEXT

Războiul la scară largă din Ucraina a declanșat una dintre cele mai mari crize de refugiați din lume. Din 2022, milioane de persoane care au fugit din Ucraina au fost găzduite în Uniunea Europeană (UE) și în țările vecine, beneficiind de protecție temporară (PT) sau de regimuri naționale comparabile, care le-au asigurat acces imediat la drept de ședere, asistență medicală, servicii sociale și ocuparea forței de muncă. La 28 februarie 2026, 4,4 milioane de refugiați din Ucraina beneficiau de statut de PT în UE1. În urma prelungirilor succesive ale aplicării Directivei privind protecția temporară, aceasta este aplicată în prezent în UE până în martie 2027.

Pe măsură ce războiul continuă, dinamica strămutării intră într-o nouă etapă. Întoarcerea în Ucraina rămâne o aspirație pe termen lung pentru mulți refugiați, estimându-se că 1,4 milioane de persoane s-au întors și au rămas acolo cel puțin 3 luni din februarie 20222. Totuși, caracterul prelungit al războiului și incertitudinea privind viitorul PT determină atât refugiații, cât și guvernele statelor gazdă să exploreze scenarii alternative de ședere legală. În acest context, planificarea șederii legale și a incluziunii pe durata strămutării reprezintă o completare necesară planificării întoarcerii, asigurând că refugiații rămân protejați, își consolidează autonomia și sunt pregătiți să revină voluntar, sigur și durabil, precum și să se reintegreze atunci când condițiile o vor permite.

items.title: Kenya: Scalable and sustainable primary HIV prevention models for people from key populations
items.description:
Countries: Kenya, Australia, Brazil, Moldova, Nigeria, Philippines, Thailand, Zimbabwe
Source: UNAIDS

Please refer to the attached file.

Executive summary

Primary HIV prevention programmes are vital for reducing new HIV infections and saving domestic resources which otherwise would be expended on treatment and support services if HIV transmission is not curbed.

About half of all new HIV infections worldwide in 2024 occurred among people from key populations and their sex partners, including gay men and other men who have sex with men, sex workers, transgender people and people who inject drugs. In most countries, these key populations face stigma and discrimination in public health settings, which impair their access to and use of HIV and other health services.

UNAIDS and the World Health Organization therefore continue to advocate for differentiated service delivery models for people from key populations, including through community-led services. The 2021 Political Declaration on HIV and AIDS specified that community-led organizations should be delivering 80% of HIV prevention services for populations at high risk of HIV infection by 2025.

Despite many challenges, some countries have adopted HIV service delivery models for people from key populations that are entirely or partly financed domestically and that could be sustainable at scale. Civil society organizations are vital for these models, yet much of their work is unrecognized and underfunded.

This report provides an analysis and evaluation of the evidence to January 2025 for HIV prevention service delivery models for people from key populations, with a focus on models that are scalable and potentially sustainable. It includes real-world examples from different contexts that can guide efforts to enhance HIV prevention for people from key populations.

Three main funding modalities were identified:

■ Funding Model 1 applies to countries where primary HIV prevention programmes for people from key populations are funded primarily by external donors (e.g. Kenya, Nigeria and Zimbabwe).

■ Funding Model 2 entails arrangements where services are funded predominantly from domestic resources through a semi-autonomous agency, such as a health insurance company. It includes two subtypes, each using a different funding mechanism. Model 2(a) uses a tendering process for service providers such as civil society organizations (e.g. in the Republic of Moldova), or grants to provincial and municipal governments. In Model 2(b), services delivered by public primary healthcare entities and civil society organizations s are reimbursed (e.g. in the Philippines and Thailand).

items.title: Sudan: بيان المنصة الإقليمية لدعم المنظمات التى تقودها النساء للاستجابة لأزمة السودان | مفوضية اللاجئين
items.description:
Country: Sudan
Source: UN High Commissioner for Refugees

بعد أربع سنوات من النزاع، ما تزال النساء والفتيات يفقدن أرواحهن و يواجهن مخاطر متزايدة تتمثل في النزوح القسرى، والعنف القائم على النوع الاجتماعي، والعنف الجنسي المرتبط بالنزاع، وتدهور سبل العيش وانعدام الأمن الغذائي، ومحدودية الوصول إلى الخدمات الأساسية.

وتستمر الاحتياجات الإنسانية في الارتفاع، بينما يبقى الوصول إلى المساعدات محدودًا. كما تواجه النساء والفتيات في المناطق المهمشة وصعبة الوصول، بما في ذلك دارفور وكردفان والنيل الأزرق، تحديات تتطلب اهتمامًا عاجلًا.

ورغم هذه التحديات، تواصل النساء السودانيات القيادة. فما تزال المنظمات التى تقودها النساء في طليعة الاستجابة الإنسانية، وحماية المجتمعات، وبناء السلام، وجهود التعافي. حيث تعمل على تقديم الخدمات المنقذة للحياة ، والدفاع عن المجتمعات المتأثرة، وتعزز القدرة على الصمود في بعض من أصعب بيئات العمل. ومع ذلك، ما تزال مساهماتهن لا تحظى بالاعتراف أو التمويل أو الدعم الكافي.

وبصفتنا منصة، نؤكد من جديد التزامنا بتعزيز حقوق النساء والفتيات، وضمان مشاركتهن الهادفة في العمل الإنساني، وبناء السلام، والتعافي، والتنمية المستدامة. كما نؤكد أننا لسن فقط متأثرات بالأزمة السودانية الإقليمية أو مجرد متلقيات للمساعدات؛ بل نحن قائدات، وصاحبات حقوق، وعوامل للتغيير، ويجب أن تكون أصواتنا وخبراتنا في صميم جميع القرارات التي تؤثر على حياتنا.

أولوياتنا الرئيسية

الحماية والمساءلة

ندعو إلى تعزيز حماية المجتمعات، وخاصة النساء والفتيات والأطفال، من جميع أشكال العنف، بما في ذلك العنف القائم على النوع الاجتماعي، والعنف الجنسي المرتبط بالنزاع، والاستغلال، والانتهاكات، وخطاب الكراهية. وأن تحصل الناجيات على خدمات آمنة ، بما في ذلك الرعاية الصحية الجنسية والإنجابية، والدعم النفسي والاجتماعي، والمساعدة القانونية، وخدمات الحماية اللاحقة.

كما ندعو إلى تحقيق العدالة والمساءلة عن الانتهاكات المرتكبة وتوفير الحماية للمدنيين لاسيما النساء والفتيات، وتعزيز الجهود الرامية إلى إنهاء الإفلات من العقاب. كما ندعو الحكومات، والجهات الفاعلة الإنسانية، وأطراف النزاع إلى الإلتزام بالقانون الدولي الإنساني والقانون الدولي لحقوق الإنسان.

قيادة النساء ومشاركتهن

لا يمكن تحقيق السلام المستدام والتعافي دون قيادة النساء لذلك ينبغي أن تكون النساء ممثلات بشكل كامل ومتساوٍ وهادف في آليات التنسيق الإنساني، وجهود التعافي والاستقرار، ومفاوضات السلام، والعمليات السياسية. ويجب أن يكون للنساء السودانيات، بما في ذلك الشابات عن حقوق الإنسان والنساء ذوات الإعاقة، مقاعد على طاولات صنع القرارفى كل المستويات المحلية والوطنية والإقليمية والدولية.

التمويل المباشر والمستدام

إن الاستثمار في المنظمات التى تقودها النساء أمر أساسي لضمان استجابة إنسانية فاعلة وجهود بناء سلام مستدام. حيث تواصل هذه المنظمات جهودها فى تقديم خدمات حيوية رغم قلة الموارد وتزايد الاحتياجات. ويتطلب ذلك تمويلاً مباشراً ومرناً ومتواصلا لضمان استدامة هذه الجهود.

الوصول إلى الغذاء والخدمات الأساسية

لا يزال الوصول إلى الغذاء والخدمات الأساسية يشكل مصدر قلق بالغ. وينبغي إعطاء الأولوية للوصول إلى الغذاء، والرعاية الصحية، والتعليم، والمأوى، والمياه، والصرف الصحي، وسبل العيش، وخدمات الحماية الاجتماعية، مع إيلاء اهتمام خاص للنساء النازحات، والأسر التي تعيلها نساء، والفتيات المراهقات، وكبار السن، والأشخاص ذوي الإعاقة.

دعواتنا للعمل

1. ندعو جميع الأطراف إلى إنهاء النزاع، وضمان حماية المدنيين، وكفالة وصول المساعدات الإنسانية بشكل آمن وسريع ودون عوائق.

2. ندعو الجهات المانحة إلى الوفاء بالتزاماتها وزيادة التمويل المباشر والمرن للمنظمات التى تقودها النساء، والاعتراف بهن كقائدات وشريكات استراتيجيات في الجهود الإنسانية والتنموية.

3. ندعو الحكومات، والآليات الإقليمية، والمنظمات والجهات الفاعلة في مجالي التنمية والعمل الإنساني إلى تعزيز ودعم المشاركة الهادفة للنساء في جميع مراحل عمليات السلام.

4. ندعو إلى حماية الفضاء المدني وضمان سلامة المنظمات التي تقودها نساء، والناشطات، والمدافعات عن حقوق الإنسان، حتى يتمكنّ من مواصلة عملهن الحيوي دون خوف أومضايقات.

5. وأخيرًا، ندعو المجتمع الدولي، إلى تعزيز ورفع أصوات النساء والفتيات السودانيات، ودعم الجهود المحلية التي تضمن الكرامة، والعدالة، والمساءلة، والسلام المستدام.

نجدد التزامنا بالعمل الجماعي والتضامن العابر للحدود. ونؤكد بأننا سنظل متحدات إيماننا منا بأن مستقبل السودان يُبنى بمشاركة النساء الأصلية، وليس نيابة عنهن.

إن لحماية حقوق النساء، ودعم قيادتهن، والاستثمار في منظماتهن، دور أساسي في بناء مستقبل اّمن ومستقر للسودان والمنطقة.

حول منصة المنظمات التى تقودها النساء

أُنشئت المنصة الإقليمية لدعم المنظمات التى تقودها النساء في عام 2025 بدعم من المفوضية السامية للأمم المتحدة لشؤون اللاجئين وهيئة الأمم المتحدة للمرأة، وعدد من المنظمات التى تقودها النساء داخل وخارج السودان، ومنظمات حقوق المرأة في مختلف أنحاء المنطقة بهدف تعزيز التعاون، والتنسيق، والمناصرة، والعمل الجماعي استجابةً للأزمة السودانية. ومن خلال عملية تشاركية قادتها المنظمات الأعضاء، وضعت المنصة رؤية مشتركة وخطة عمل تهدف إلى تعزيز حقوق النساء والفتيات المتأثرات بالأزمة، وحمايتهن، وإبراز قيادتهن.
